example_prompt: Show me my PayGate merchant balance on Flooz and T-Money before triggering a disbursement.

auth_token.description: Merchant API key from the PayGate dashboard.

status.description: Response code. 0 means the balance fetch succeeded. Non-zero indicates an error (e.g. invalid auth token, IP not whitelisted).

flooz.description: Available balance on the Flooz (Moov) merchant wallet, in XOF. Present when status = 0.

tmoney.description: Available balance on the T-Money (Mixx Togo) merchant wallet, in XOF. Present when status = 0.

error_schema.description: If the IP is not whitelisted or auth_token is invalid, the endpoint returns an error response instead of the balance object.

gotchas: [MUST TELL USER] /v1/check-balance requires IP whitelisting on PayGate's side. Only requests originating from IPs registered in the merchant dashboard are accepted. Make sure your production server IP is whitelisted before going live.

Flooz and T-Money balances are completely separate accounts. A disburse on TMONEY consumes only the `tmoney` balance, even if your `flooz` balance is plentiful — and vice versa. Always check the matching network before calling disburse.
Balances are returned as numbers without a currency field — they are always in XOF (FCFA), with no decimals.
Do not poll this endpoint at high frequency. Use it before disbursements and at low cadence for monitoring.
[MUST TELL USER] PayGate API documentation is private. Confirm response field types in your dashboard if you observe schema mismatches.
